title: centaurea salonitana subsp. ognjanoffii
intro: Centaurea salonitana subsp. ognjanoffii, a rare and lesser-known subspecies of Centaurea, is a flowering plant native to the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Eastern Europe. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Centaurea salonitana subsp. ognjanoffii include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and protect against infections, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. Traditionally, this plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems.
